name: Ability Score Improvement
summary: A universal level-up reward, not a class feature — every class gets it at the same levels.

sections.heading: What it is
paragraphs: An Ability Score Improvement (ASI) lets you raise your ability scores as you level. It is the standard way a character's numbers climb over time: a higher primary ability means better attack and damage rolls, higher save DCs on the effects you force, and stronger saving throws of your own.
This is a base rule of the system. It applies identically to every class, so it lives here in the Rules rather than being printed as a feature on each class sheet.

sections.heading: When you gain one
paragraphs: Every class gains an Ability Score Improvement at 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level. (Levels beyond 5 are outside the current build scope but the cadence is fixed here so it never has to be re-decided per class.)

sections.heading: What you may do with it
list: Increase one ability score by 2, or
Increase two ability scores by 1 each.
No ability score can go above 20 this way.
If your table uses feats, you may take a feat instead of the score increase.
